title: Writing - Part 1: Homelessness
description: Your class has been discussing the problem of homelessness and the most effective ways to address it in modern cities.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the solutions in your notes. You should explain which solution you think is most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Without safe and stable accommodation, it is almost impossible for homeless people to rebuild their lives.
Many homeless people need access to mental health, addiction and healthcare services before they can move on.
Helping people find work gives them financial independence and a sense of purpose.

structure.intro: Introduce the homelessness problem and state the two solutions you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first solution and how it could help homeless people.
structure.body2: Discuss the second solution and compare its effectiveness to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which solution you regard as most important and why.

content.modelAnswer:
Homelessness is a growing problem in cities around the world, reflecting deep inequalities in housing, healthcare and social support. Two of the most important solutions are expanding affordable housing provision and improving access to support services.

Providing affordable housing is arguably the most fundamental step. Research consistently shows that stable accommodation is the prerequisite for all other aspects of recovery. Schemes such as Housing First, which prioritises giving homeless people a permanent home before addressing other issues, have produced impressive results in several countries. Without a safe place to live, it is extremely difficult for individuals to address health or employment challenges.

However, many homeless people have complex needs, including mental health conditions or substance dependency, that cannot be resolved by housing alone. Access to professional support services, including counselling, medical care and addiction programmes, is essential to help people rebuild their lives sustainably.

In my opinion, affordable housing must be the priority, as it provides the stable foundation from which everything else can follow. Without secure accommodation, support services can only offer temporary relief. Combining both approaches in an integrated strategy would offer the most effective and humane response to homelessness.
